title: AutoQuant Skill Definition
description: Schema for skill definitions used by the AutoQuant skill system. This file is documentation-only for now and does not enable runtime skill loading.

id.description: Unique slug for the skill. Used as a registry key and in identifiers. Must be lowercase, filesystem-safe, and URL-safe.

version.description: Semver string for the skill definition itself, not the underlying AutoQuant system version.

description.description: Plain-language explanation of what the skill does, when it activates, and what happens on failure.

enabled.description: Master on/off switch. Disabled skills must be completely ignored by future runtime loaders.

stages.description: AutoQuant pipeline stage identifiers where this skill is allowed to activate.

gates.description: Optional finer-grained activation hooks within a stage.

applies_to.description: Declares where the skill is allowed to run. If this does not match the current run phase, the skill must be ignored.

type.description: The root type of skill runtime parameters. Currently only object-shaped configs are supported.

required.description: Names of properties that must be present in the resolved run config for this skill.

type.description: Allowed parameter types. Use string only for labels and documentation values.

properties.description: Declares allowed runtime parameters and their validation rules for this skill.

config.description: Parameter contract for the skill. Backends must validate runtime config against this before executing skill logic.

tags.description: Optional categorization labels for filtering and documentation.

author.description: Optional display-only author or owner string.

dependencies.description: Optional list of other skill ids that should be active before this skill runs.

preconditions.description: Optional boolean expressions evaluated against run state before activation. Missing means the skill activates whenever applies_to matches and it is enabled.

rollback.description: Optional retry behavior. Missing means no rollout/rollback is permitted.

timeout_seconds.description: Optional hard execution timeout in seconds. If missing, the pipeline default timeout applies.

permissions.description: Capability contract for the skill. Missing or untrue means the skill is read-only and cannot alter strategy source or run state.

docs.description: Optional relative path to markdown documentation for this skill.
